mechanism_narrative: A sustained decline in the federal funds rate reduces the discount rate applied to future corporate earnings, directly compressing equity valuation multiples and generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the subsequent month.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in easing periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate falls below 5.0 percent, consumer confidence and wage growth expectations improve, boosting aggregate demand and driving positive S&P 500 forward returns within one week.
failure_condition: SPX produces negative 5-day returns in more than 60% of instances where UNRATE crosses below 5.0%, or the signal shows no predictive power after controlling for FEDFUNDS changes.

mechanism_narrative: Rising industrial production combined with declining housing starts signals a supply-demand imbalance that fuels cost-push inflation, forcing the Federal Reserve to tighten policy and depressing equity valuations over the next month.
failure_condition: The joint signal fails to predict negative 20-day returns in more than 50% of triggered periods, or the interaction term shows no statistical significance in a multivariate regression over a 5-year sample.

mechanism_narrative: Accelerating consumer price inflation erodes real corporate profit margins and triggers anticipatory monetary tightening, which compresses equity risk premiums and drives negative S&P 500 forward returns over a quarterly horizon.
failure_condition: Quarterly forward returns remain positive or neutral when CPIAUCSL accelerates above 3.0% annualized, or the signal's predictive power vanishes after adjusting for FEDFUNDS trajectory.

mechanism_narrative: Expanding real consumer credit balances indicate rising household leverage and spending capacity, which directly supports corporate revenue growth and generates positive S&P 500 forward returns within five trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces negative 5-day returns in more than 65% of instances where RSAFS shows sequential monthly growth, or the signal exhibits no out-of-sample Rank IC above 0.02.

mechanism_narrative: A contraction in industrial production exceeding 1.5 percent over three months signals weakening manufacturing demand and corporate earnings downgrades, triggering negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Equity returns remain positive or flat during confirmed INDPRO contractions of this magnitude, or the signal fails to outperform a random baseline in a 3-year walk-forward test.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous growth in total nonfarm payrolls and a declining unemployment rate confirms a self-reinforcing labor market expansion, which sustains consumer spending and drives positive S&P 500 forward returns over a quarterly period.
failure_condition: The joint PAYEMS/UNRATE signal predicts negative returns in more than 50% of confirmed labor market expansions, or the interaction coefficient is statistically indistinguishable from zero over a 10-year sample.

mechanism_narrative: Rising housing starts act as a leading indicator for construction activity, durable goods demand, and household wealth effects, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns within one week during a housing recovery phase.
failure_condition: SPX generates negative 5-day returns in more than 60% of months where HOUST shows sequential growth, or the signal's predictive accuracy drops below 50% in a rolling 2-year window.
